# Copilot Instructions for Mathijs

## Overview

This repository contains the Mathijs project. When contributing, Copilot should follow the guidelines below to ensure consistent and high-quality code.

## General Guidelines

- Write clear, readable, and maintainable code
- Follow established coding conventions and patterns already present in the codebase
- Include meaningful comments where necessary to explain complex logic
- Prefer simple, straightforward solutions over complex ones

## Code Quality

- Ensure code is well-structured and follows best practices
- Write self-documenting code with descriptive variable and function names
- Keep functions focused and single-purpose
- Handle errors appropriately and provide meaningful error messages

## Documentation

- Update documentation when making changes that affect usage or behavior
- Include inline comments for complex or non-obvious code sections
- Ensure README and other docs stay up to date with new features

## Testing

- Write tests for new functionality when test infrastructure exists
- Ensure existing tests pass before submitting changes
- Consider edge cases when writing or modifying code

## Commits and Pull Requests

- Write clear, descriptive commit messages
- Keep changes focused and atomic
- Provide context in pull request descriptions
